$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for `help_docs.upsertByPath` (the deferred
path-keyed upsert on the help-docs repo). One committed file drives BOTH
sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-help-docs-upsert-fixture.ts + help-docs-upsert-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness help_docs upsert tier-2 test).
MINTED-VALUES (REMAP) FORM. Unlike the pinned help-docs tier-2 case, the
upsert ops pin NOTHING: v4's `upsertByPath` mints its own id + timestamps on
the create branch and mints `updatedAt` on the update branch, and the Rust
port mints its own (different) values. The harness reconciles the two dumps by
remapping `id` to first-seen tokens (rows sorted by the natural key `path`)
and placeholdering `createdAt`/`updatedAt`. Every OTHER column (including the
`embedding` hex/null) is compared exactly.
SEED rows ARE pinned (id + timestamps) so the starting state is deterministic.
Seed row 1 (`help/aurora.md`) carries a NON-NULL embedding so the test proves
a text-only upsert-update leaves the BLOB intact; the dump compares it as hex.
Seed row 2 (`help/prospero.md`) has a NULL embedding and is never touched by
any op (its presence proves untouched seed rows survive).
EMBEDDING VALUES are chosen to be EXACTLY float32-representable so the
f64->f32 cast is lossless and identical on both sides. [0.5,-0.25,0.75,0.125]
encodes little-endian as `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`.
OPS (all upsertByPath):
1. path help/aurora.md -> matches seed row 1 -> UPDATE (new title/url/content/
contentHash). The embedding BLOB MUST stay -> dump still shows the hex.
2. path help/pascal.md  -> no match -> CREATE (minted id+ts, embedding NULL).
3. path help/pascal.md  -> matches the row created in op 2 -> UPDATE of a
minted-id row (the create-then-update-same-path case).
4. path help/carina.md  -> no match -> CREATE (minted id+ts, embedding NULL).
FINAL state (4 rows): aurora (updated, embedding intact), carina (created, NULL),
pascal (created then updated, NULL), prospero (seed, untouched).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the other
Phase-2 fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.

ops.comment: Matches seed row 1 (help/aurora.md) -> UPDATE. Changes title/url/content/contentHash. The non-null embedding BLOB must survive untouched (dump still shows 0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e). updatedAt minted by upsertByPath.

data.title: Aurora overview, revised
data.content: Aurora is the dawn engine, rewritten.

ops.comment: No existing row for help/pascal.md -> CREATE with minted id + timestamps. embedding is NULL (upsertByPath's create branch never sets it).
Matches the row created in op 2 (help/pascal.md) -> UPDATE of a MINTED-id row. embedding stays NULL. updatedAt minted.

data.title: Pascal overview, revised
data.content: Pascal keeps the ledger, now balanced.

ops.comment: No existing row for help/carina.md -> CREATE with minted id + timestamps. embedding is NULL.
